channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Bushido Beneath The Open Sky Themed Warband Review - We Ride!
item.content:encoded:
A new faction for Bushido rides into the fray! The Horse Lords of the Ataka Plains have returned, having kept themselves apart from the feuding clans. They have now pledged their allegiance to the Prefecture of Ryu, but rumors of a curse circulate, so all may not be as it seems. In this article, we take a look at the new Beneath the Open Sky warband box and the new Horse Lords faction for Bushido.

We've been covering Bushido for years, so if you'd like to learn more about the game, check out our guide to Bushido Risen Sun, our review of the Bushido Risen Sun 2-player starter set, and our "Start Collecting" guide to Bushido, which includes an interview with the game's developer.

Thematically Themed

When selecting a warband in Bushido, you must first choose a faction from the now 15 available to select your fighters from. Each faction also has access to theme cards, which offer special abilities but restrict the units that can be included in the warband. For example, the Horse Lords are a new faction for Bushido, and Cavalry Charge is one of their theme cards that restricts you to Samurai fighters, but gives them additional benefits.

The Horse Lords currently have 2 themes, Cavalry Charge and Hit & Run. Both limit fighters to only Samurai, but Cavalry Charge makes it harder for your opponents to pass a fear test (which the Horse Lords can cause with equipment cards at their disposal), and Hit & Run gives them additional movement benefits and allows the first friendly model each turn to only take half damage after a successful charge attack roll.

When taking either of these 2 theme cards, you have to take 1 of the 3 currently available Event Curse cards as well. These cards cost -10 rice, giving you more to spend when building your warband, but have a negative effect like Beyond the Plains, which lets your opponent set up all of the terrain, or Misfortune, which means you automatically lose the Tactical Roll on odd-numbered turns.

Ride Or Die

The current wave of Horse Lords' miniatures are all mounted, which shouldn't come as a surprise, and they're all pretty rapid. This speed doesn't come from their movement stat, which isn't much different from non-mounted fighters, but from the Cavalry trait, which gives them a bonus movement that they can do before or after any action,  boosting their standard movement, or letting them move slightly before doing anything else.

Each Horse Lords fighter is fairly expensive, so you're not going to be outnumbering anyone, and as a Shadow Wind Clan player, I know the issues this can cause. It makes the warband fairly unforgiving when it comes to activations. You have to make sure that every action counts, but they are pretty effective in combat, especially when charging, and are all durable and obviously extremely mobile.

Lords of the Horse

The Horse Lords, Beneath The Open Sky warband box, contains 5 models. These are:

  • Oiwa Yoshitaka - Oiwa is a powerful fighter, with 4 melee dice and a +1 damage katana with a Pierce 1 charging bonus and a melee prowess bonus which allows him to reroll 1 dice in combat. Oiwa's most efficient use is to pull friendly models into a melee exchange with the Mouth of Jigoku, once per game, ki feat, which allows you to move through models and ignore zones of control when charging. Any friendly models moved through in this way may also be placed into base-to-base contact with the charge target, along with removing a whole host of negative markers too. The other models won't get their charge bonus when they attack later, but it will allow you to overwhelm a chosen target and then move on to the next.
  • Tomoto Kazuma - Tomoto is another powerful fighter, with 4 melee dice and a +1 damage katana with a Pierce 1 charging bonus and melee prowess 1. He doesn't have Oiwa's powerful charge ki feat, but he does have the once-per-game Fade ki feat, which allows him to leave the battlefield and return using the flank rules, which, with his cavalry movement, allows him to threaten in unexpected areas.
  • Taka Toshuyuki - Taka has Armor 3, where the others only have Armor 2 and a slightly lower melee pool of 3. His Yari also doesn't have +1 like the katana's of Oiwa and Tomoto, but it does have Reach, which gives Taka the initiative in melee if not in base-to-base contact with their opponent at the start of the activation. Making Taka more effective on the charge, he also gives his target a Stunned marker whenever entering base-to-base contact after a charge, reducing their melee pool by 1, and can also use the Do and Die ki feat, once per turn, to give another friendly fighter Coordinated attack, which boosts the melee pool if they're in combat with another fighter.
  • Inoue Kikuko - Inoue is the ranged fighter for the Beneath the Open Sky warband, armed with a +1 damage Dai Kyu, and a boosted cavalry trait of 3 inches, making them extremely rapid for positioning around the battlefield. Their Dai Kyu has reload 1, but Inoue has a Quick Reload ki feat to help them remove markers and keep firing. But before that, they can also use the once per game Zen Archery ki feat to make a second ranged attack, immediately following the first, ignoring reload markers to load up on damage on a target before the rest of the warband charges in
  • Watanabe Yuka - Watanabe is young and aggressive with a standard move of 5 inches, topped up by Cavalry 2. Watanabe's Nagamaki has +1 damage, along with Pierce 2 and Slam Attack on the charge, which pushes their target away and causes extra damage. Watanabe has Impetuous 0, which doesn't do anything on its own, but if she uses her Adrenaline Rush ki feat, which grants her Intangible to allow them to move through anything on the charge, and also Unblockable 1, which forces your opponent to discard their highest defence dice, then they gain an Impetuous Marker at the start of the next turn, which means they have to activate first next turn, but are Fearless until they activate again. Watanabe also has a Vitality ki feat that can be used once per turn to gain another activation counter to really harass your opponent in combat.

Adding To The Charge

Releasing alongside Beneath the Open Sky is Takeda Reiko, who is insanely fast with a standard movement of 5 and Cavalry 4, to let them cover some serious distance each turn. Takeda also has a unique ability that lets her charge immediately after using her command trait to make 3 other fighters charge, without using another activation to charge herself. This can be combined with her Honor the Charge ki feat, which can be used once per game to give Takeda Intangible, and can charge the alignment by 1 of any objectives moved through on the charge.

What Are Our Final Thoughts On The Beneath The Open Sky Themed Warband?

Beneath the Open Sky is another great thematic warband for Bushido, and really lands the Horse Lords as a faction to be reckoned with. Incredibly fast, and with abilities that let several move together, they can land some overwhelmingly devastating attacks. They do suffer as a low model count warband, but their damage output, durability, and movement speed offset it slightly. Having them charge down a single target and then wheel around to find the next is incredibly rewarding, and the curse events really add to the flavor of the faction. We ride!

The copy of Bushido Beneath the Open Sky used to produce this review was provided by GCT Studios.

Adam is a Tabletop Specialist for TechRaptor. He started writing for TechRaptor in 2017 and took over as Tabletop Editor in 2019 and has since stood down… More about Adam



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/bushido-beneath-open-sky-themed-warband-review-we-ride
